reasoning: Over a 4-day trading period (accounting for the Good Friday market holiday on April 3), stock price movements for a non-earnings week closely follow a random walk with a very slight upward drift. The YFinance mathematical model based on trend predicted an approximately 52% chance of exceeding the current price. I disregard the seasonal indicator (which boosted it to 59%) because relying on a single prior year is statistically insignificant and prone to noise. Therefore, a generic base rate of 52-53% for an arbitrary stock closing higher over a short, quiet trading week is the safest assumption.
